items.headline: Harry Kane doubles saves England against Congo DR at World Cup
items.description: Harry Kane's brace saved England from World Cup humiliation as Thomas Tuchel's men survived an almighty scare against Congo DR.

items.headline: World Cup Daily live: Kane rescues England in comeback win over Congo DR
items.description: We have three matches for you on Wednesday as more round-of-32 action at the 2026 World Cup continues.

items.headline: Brazil's Lucas Paquetá to undergo intensive treatment on hamstring injury in bid to play at World Cup again
items.description: Lucas Paquetá will undergo intensive treatment on a hamstring injury in the hope of playing for Brazil at the World Cup again.

items.headline: Chancellor of Germany derided for praising 'team spirit' of national side after World Cup exit
items.description: The Chancellor of Germany has been subject to derision online following a tweet in which he lauded his national team's "team spirit" in their World Cup elimination at the hands of Paraguay.

items.headline: Marcus Rashford, Noni Madueke start for England vs. Congo DR at World Cup
items.description: Thomas Tuchel has made three changes for England's round of 32 World Cup knockout match against Congo DR in Atlanta, with Marcus Rashford retaining his place on the left wing, while Noni Madueke, Djed Spence and Declan Rice all start.

items.headline: Marcelo Bielsa on 'painful' Uruguay World Cup exit: 'Nobody cares what I know'
items.description: Marcelo Bielsa has said that "nobody was interested" in what he transmitted after Uruguay were knocked out of the World Cup.

items.headline: Orlando Pirates confirm Relebohile Mofokeng transfer agreement with Belgium's RUSG
items.description: Orlando Pirates have reached an agreement with Royale Union Saint-Gilloise for the transfer of Relebohile Mofokeng pending personal terms and a medical.

items.headline: French defender Jérémy Jacquet officially joins Liverpool from Rennes
items.description: Liverpool have completed the signing of France U21 centre-back Jérémy Jacquet from Rennes in a deal worth up to £60million.

items.headline: Ranking all 20 Premier League clubs on transfer work for 2025-26 season
items.description: One year on from a bumper 2025 summer Premier League window, we've decided to rate all 20 teams' incoming signings on their immediate impact and scope to improve.

items.headline: Transfer rumors, news: USMNT's Pulisic on six-man Liverpool shortlist
items.description: USMNT star Christian Pulisic could be headed back to the Premier League. Transfer Talk has the latest.

items.headline: Mexico send a World Cup message from 'Fortress Azteca' as fans aid win over Ecuador
items.description: The Estadio Azteca was deafening on Tuesday night as Mexico picked up their first win in a World Cup knockout match in 40 years.

items.headline: Every team's championship odds for the 2026 World Cup
items.description: Odds for every team to advance through the knockout stage and to win the 2026 World Cup.

items.headline: Riding high at a World Cup on home soil, the time is now for the USMNT
items.description: At a World Cup on home soil, the USMNT boasts a world-class coach and star players in their primes. This is its chance to make its mark on world soccer.

items.headline: World Cup: One big question for every round-of-16 team
items.description: As this week sees the World Cup field cut from 32 to 16, we're wondering what the biggest issue is for each team still in the tournament -- like can France win if they don't control possession?

items.headline: World Cup 2026: Can Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo finally meet on biggest stage?
items.description: In a sixth World Cup for Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, could the pair finally face each other in the tournament?

items.headline: World Cup: Grades for every eliminated team as they exit the tournament
items.description: When the World Cup ends, 47 teams will have been eliminated, and we're grading every one on how their run compared with expectations.

items.headline: Germany's Manuel Neuer retires from international football, has 'no regrets' after World Cup exit
items.description: Bayern Munich captain Manuel Neuer insists he has "no regrets" after announcing his decision to retire from international duty - for the second time in his career - following Germany's "very painful" World Cup exit.

items.headline: Three people die from suffocation during Mexico's celebrations post-Ecuador win in World Cup
items.description: Three people have died from asphyxiation in Mexico City during World Cup celebrations, local authorities confirmed on Wednesday.

items.headline: Mexico boss: WC knockout win vs. Ecuador a 'perfect night'

items.headline: Assessing Scotland's post-World Cup manager contenders: Ange Postecoglou? David Moyes? Kasper Hjulmand?
items.description: ESPN's Mark Donaldson looks over the options available to the Scottish FA as they work to replace Steve Clarke after the team's World Cup elimination.

items.headline: Socceroos under no illusions ahead of Egypt: 'Eat or be eaten'
items.description: Australia great respect for Mohamed Salah and Egypt, but Socceroos flyer Jordan Bos says that the team is under no illusions that Friday's FIFA World Cup round of 32 clash is anything other than "eat or be eaten."

items.headline: Jordan Pickford: England 'prepared' for World Cup penalty shootout
items.description: England have a plan in place if their round of 32 match against Congo DR goes to a dreaded penalty shootout, and Jordan Pickford says England are ready for any scenario.

items.headline: What it feels like to pull off a World Cup upset, from Paraguay to Cape Verde
items.description: Paraguay's win over Germany in the round of 32 is the latest World Cup shock in a long line of them, including famous upsets like Ireland over Italy in 1994 and Norway over Brazil in 1998. What's it feel like to beat a giant?

items.headline: Mexico oust Ecuador for 1st World Cup knockout win in 40 years
items.description: Mexico won their first World Cup knockout stage match in 40 years after dispatching Ecuador 2-0 in front an electric crowd at Estadio Azteca on Tuesday.

items.headline: FIFA identifies 13-fold rise in abusive social media posts towards World Cup players
items.description: A 13-fold increase in abusive social media posts directed towards World Cup participants compared to the last tournament four years ago has been identified by football's world governing body FIFA.

items.headline: Opposition scouting: Bosnia and Herzegovina's set piece specialty will require USMNT's full focus
items.description: The U.S. has already succeeded against defensive-minded opponents, but will that continue in the last 32 against Bosnia and Herzegovina?

items.headline: Mbappé targeting winning WC, not Golden Boot race with Messi

items.headline: Inside the Socceroos camp as they prepare for Egypt
items.description: As history beckons for the Socceroos, they must find a way to harness the motivation from the scale of the task at hand, pay heed to the approach that got them there, and make sure that the players play the game, and not the sense of occasion.

items.headline: Sebastián Beccacece steps down as Ecuador coach after World Cup exit
items.description: Sebastián Beccacece has confirmed he will not continue as head coach of Ecuador following the team's elimination from the World Cup.

items.headline: World Cup 2026: Are we witnessing the end of this great Senegal team?
items.description: It's not over, not by any stretch: they have the quality, the firepower to defeat Iraq by a large margin and give themselves a chance to progress.

items.headline: Are European champions Spain still among World Cup favorites?
items.description: They may have booked a round-of-32 tie vs. Austria as group winners, but Spain's performances raised doubts about the team and coach Luis de la Fuente.
